name: R Seurat Visium spot/image overlay v2
description: Fail-closed Seurat Spatial identity or feature overlay with barcode-coordinate-scale reconciliation and declared typography, palette, legend, label, spot, and image controls.

parameters.name: object
parameters.description: Trusted caller-supplied object, bound by the input artifact and never modified.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: SpatialDimPlot identities/metadata or SpatialFeaturePlot assay features.

parameters.name: assay
parameters.description: Spatial-registration assay associated with the image.

parameters.name: feature_assay
parameters.description: Existing normalized assay for feature values; null uses assay.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: One image; required explicitly when the assay has multiple images.

parameters.name: group_by
parameters.description: Metadata grouping for identity mode; null uses active identities.

parameters.name: features
parameters.description: Existing assay features for feature mode; values are not recomputed.

parameters.name: identity_palette
parameters.description: Optional categorical identity palette.

parameters.name: feature_palette
parameters.allowed: at least two colours in low-to-high order
parameters.description: Continuous feature palette; reversing scale semantics requires scientific confirmation.

parameters.name: crop
parameters.description: Use Seurat's registered tissue crop without reconstructing coordinates.

parameters.name: label
parameters.description: Add existing identity labels in identity mode.

parameters.name: label_size
parameters.description: Purely visual identity-label size.

parameters.name: label_repel
parameters.description: Use Seurat label repulsion in identity mode.

parameters.name: pt_size_factor
parameters.description: Purely visual spot-size factor.

parameters.name: spot_alpha
parameters.description: Purely visual spot opacity.

parameters.name: image_alpha
parameters.description: Purely visual histology opacity.

parameters.name: image_scale
parameters.description: Existing image scale with a validated positive factor.

parameters.name: base_size
parameters.description: Base typography size.

parameters.name: legend_position
parameters.description: Position of existing categorical or continuous guides.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Optional descriptive title.

transformations.method: Seurat GetTissueCoordinates with declared image scale
existing data layer from explicitly selected assay
Seurat SpatialDimPlot or SpatialFeaturePlot without manual coordinate reconstruction

supports_claims: Shows displayed identities or feature values at registered spots in the selected tissue image.
does_not_support: Does not establish spatial enrichment significance, registration accuracy beyond contract checks, cell-type deconvolution, biological replication, population effects, mechanism, or causality.
required_statistics: none for descriptive overlay; inference requires separate replicate-aware spatial statistics
safer_alternatives: separate tissue image and quantitative table when overlay occlusion prevents reading

visual_fingerprint.final_size_behavior: requires spot-alignment, legend, tissue-detail and clipping checks
visual_fingerprint.review_note: The final v2 code rendered identity and Hpca/Ttr feature overlays from the hash-bound 2,695-spot Visium object. All four original/final-size images passed native review; labels are expression-derived spot clusters, not cell types.

source.transformation: original v2 rewrite with explicit visual parameter contract

validation.runtime: R-4.5.3; Seurat-5.5.0; SeuratObject-5.4.0; ggplot2-4.0.3; patchwork-1.3.2; renv-1.2.2; Bioconductor-3.21
notes: A fresh final-code execution reconciled all 2,695 Spatial-assay, image and coordinate barcodes with zero six-way set differences before rendering.

Identity and feature paths use official Seurat spatial plotting functions; the feature path replaces only the returned continuous fill colours and does not alter values, limits, transformations, coordinates or spot selection.
All four v2 original/final previews received terminal KEEP decisions in native review; see validation-evidence.json.
No v1 visual evidence, failed pre-fix render or warning-emitting intermediate implementation is reused as v2 release evidence.
